title: Nap Lagard
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Character |type=Corporate |image= |name=Nap Lagard |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]]''[[Cloak of Deception]]'' |gender= |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|They may think they have struck a blow against the Trade Federation. But they will think again when Dorvalla is forced to make restitution to us.|Nap Lagard, speaking of the engagement over Dorvalla|}} '''Nap Lagard''' was a [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] [[Starship/Legends|ship]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] for the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]]. In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], he commanded the [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class freighter]] [[Acquisitor (Lucrehulk-class battleship)|''Acquisitor'']], which was stationed in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. During that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], the Trade Federation Lucrehulk [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Revenue]]'' came under [[Dorvalla Incident|attack]] by [[Nebula Front/Legends|Nebula Front]] [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorists]] over the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dorvalla/Legends|Dorvalla]]. Lagard answered a [[Distress signal/Legends|distress call]] sent by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Daultay Dofine/Legends|Daultay Dofine]] and managed to fight off the Nebula Front with the ''Acquisitor''. After picking up Dofine from an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]], Lagard took part in a conversation with Trade Federation [[Viceroy/Legends|Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]], which resulted in Dofine taking over command of the ''Acquisitor''. ==Biography== {{Quote|Lagard, listen closely. Contact Viceroy Gunray. It is urgent that I speak with him immediately.''"
"''Viceroy Gunray? What is so urgent?''"
"''That is for the Viceroy alone to hear.|Daultay Dofine rebuffs Nap Lagard during the engagement over Dorvalla|}} [[File:Daultay_Dofine.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Daultay Dofine, whom Lagard rescued over Dorvalla and who later replaced Lagard as captain of the ''Acquisitor'']] Nap Lagard was a [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] who served the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] during the final [[Standard year/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Cloak of Deception]]'' to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. he was in [[Commander/Legends|command]] of the heavily-armed [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class freighter]] [[Acquisitor (Lucrehulk-class battleship)|''Acquisitor'']], which was stationed in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. During that [[Time/Legends|time]], the ''[[Revenue]]'', another Trade Federation Lucrehulk [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] loaded with a cache of priceless [[Aurodium/Legends|aurodium]] [[ingot]]s, was [[Dorvalla Incident|attacked]] over the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dorvalla/Legends|Dorvalla]] by [[Nebula Front/Legends|Nebula Front]], an organization of [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorists]]. [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Daultay Dofine/Legends|Daultay Dofine]] of the ''Revenue'' sent a [[Distress signal/Legends|distress call]] for help, and Lagard moved his [[Starship/Legends|ship]] to the [[Dorvalla system/Legends|Dorvalla system]] to fight off the Nebula Front [[Trooper/Legends|troops]] and rescue as much of the ''Revenue''{{'s}} cargo as possible. After arriving at the Dorvalla [[Star system/Legends|system]], Lagard and his well-armed ship quickly fought off the Nebula Front forces, except for the [[Hawk-Bat (gunship)|''Hawk-Bat'']], the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of the Nebula Front-aligned [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Arwen Cohl]]. After the ''Hawk-Bat'' retreated too, a [[Unidentified Neimoidian subordinate|subordinate]] informed Lagard that they received a signal from Dofine's [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]]. Dofine instantly ordered Lagard to contact Trade Federation [[Viceroy/Legends|Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]], although he did not tell Lagard why he should do so. Dofine also told Lagard that Cohl, who had boarded the ''Revenue'' during the battle and stolen its cargo before leaving in another escape pod, had set the ship to explode. After the ''Acquisitor'' picked up Dofine, Lagard himself—accompanied by a [[Mechno-chair/Legends|mechno-chair]] to communicate with Gunray—greeted Dofine. In the ensuing conversation, Dofine reported the loss of the ''Revenue'' and its cargo. However, Gunray ordered Dofine to take over command of the ''Acquisitor'' from Lagard and leave the area. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|The viceroy is eager to hear what you have to report. As am I, Commander.|Nap Lagard, upon rescuing Daultay Dofine|}} Like most of the Trade Federation members, Nap Lagard hated the Nebula Front and was eager to end the threat the group posed. To that end, he intended to force Dorvalla to pay compensation for the attack on the Trade Federation. The Commander was curious about the news that Dofine wanted to report to Gunray, and Dofine rebuffed him for asking for the news. Lagard reacted quickly by asking his colleague a humiliating question about the fate of Dofine's ship, albeit knowing that Dofine had already lost the ''Revenue''. ==Skills and abilities== Lagard was able to command a Trade Federation ship, and he successfully drove back the Nebula Front forces over Dorvalla. ==Behind the scenes== Nap Lagard first appeared in ''[[Cloak of Deception]]'', a [[2001]] novel written by [[James Luceno]]. The name "Nap Lagard" was mentioned in the "Standard Game" mode of ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', a 2001 [[Star Wars video games|video game]] by [[LucasArts]], as well as its [[2002]] sequel, ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]'', in which he is one of the randomly chosen leaders for the Trade Federation. However, as he does not actually appear in the original ''Galactic Battlegrounds'', and the mention of "Nap Lagard" is only a randomly chosen name, this article treats his appearance in the game as [[Canon|non-canon]].''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' {{Nc}} {{Gamecameo}} *''[[Cloak of Deception]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]'' {{Nc}} {{Gamecameo}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Nap Lagard |pl=Nap Lagard |fi=Nap Lagard |es=Nap Lagard }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Lagard, Nap}} [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Neimoidians]] [[Category:Trade Federation members]]
